headline: Tesla Has Only 69 Robotaxis In Texas — But They Are Cheap
summary: Tesla Inc. is getting lapped in the Texas robotaxi race. The question is whether it matters. Texas Department of Motor Vehicles data as of June 16 puts Tesla’s statewide fleet at just 69 vehicles, according to research from Bank of America. Waymo leads with 620, AVRide has 317, Nuro and Zoox round out the field at 47 and 35. Tesla is also trails its competitors in markets. The company is live in four cities, with five more listed as “preparations underway,” while Waymo operates in 11. Tesla is s

headline: A SpaceX-Tesla Merger Could Manufacture A $5 Trillion Giant: Here's What Prediction Markets Say
summary: Elon Musk’s next move could be a SpaceX-Tesla merger big enough to challenge Nvidia Corp. for the title of most valuable company on Earth. Just three sessions into its IPO, SpaceX has already surged nearly 50% to roughly $2.6 trillion, leaving Tesla Inc. and its $1.5 trillion behind. The wider that gap runs, the cheaper Tesla gets to absorb, and the more control Musk keeps over whatever emerges. The Math On A $5 Trillion Giant The case for a merger, laid out this week by Bloomberg columnist Liam

headline: Tesla (TSLA) Faces EU FSD Split As Musk Lifts Voting Stake To 20%
summary: Sweden has urged the EU to block Tesla’s Full Self-Driving rollout unless the feature that lets vehicles exceed speed limits is disabled, as an EU-wide vote approaches. Denmark has tentatively cleared FSD under Dutch regulatory rules, creating a split in European treatment of Tesla’s driver-assistance software. Elon Musk has converted stock options into voting shares, increasing his voting stake in Tesla to 20% and tightening his grip on corporate decision-making. Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) sits...

headline: No one wants AI data centers on Earth. Do they make sense in space?
